learning_outcomes.description: Critically and reflexively analyse workplace tasks and apply effective problem-solving in real-world contexts
Communicate workplace insights confidently and respond effectively to questions and feedback
Evaluate peers' work and develop feedback skills aligned with industry practice
Identify and reflect on learning gained through the internship and articulate how it can be applied to future professional contexts.

assessments.assessment_title: Case Study: Identify a problem in the workplace
assessments.category: Essay/ Critique, Participation/ Student contribution, Placement
assessments.task_description:
Identify a problem in the workplace.

Identify a problem or opportunity in the workplace where you are undertaking your internship. It could be a system that could be streamlined, a lack of documentation in some aspect of work flow, or an idea for software or equipment that would make things easier for management or employees. Write up a 700-800 word proposal.

If a submission does not adhere to the specified word RANGE, the content of the submission must be assessed and the element of non-compliance marked through a separate criterion (rubric), and the assessment marked up to the specified word limit. Please refer to Blackboard for full instructions.

The next part of this assessment includes conducting a Peer Review of 2 x peer case studies as instructed in Blackboard by the date of the in-person Assessment Workshop. Finally, attend the Assessment Workshop to respond, in-person, to peer feedback and questions. (Note, you're not being assessed on your in-person performance, only on the content of your case study).
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submit via TurnItIn Link and Review peer work through the same link via the PeerMark tab.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
Workplace Reflection

Students are required to submit a reflective essay of 2100-2300 words (excluding references, appendices or work-related extracts) based on their experiences and observations during their internship. The essay is an opportunity to critically evaluate your professional development, reflect on workplace tasks, and connect your experience to industry practices and emerging technologies.


If a submission does not adhere to the specified word RANGE, the content of the submission must be assessed and the element of non-compliance marked through a separate criterion (rubric), and the assessment marked up to the specified word limit. Please refer to Blackboard for full instructions.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Assignment to be submitted electronically via Blackboard and using TurnItIn. Before submitting any assignments for this course you must ensure you have completed UQ's compulsory online Academic Integrity Tutorial.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.
